evals.prompt:
Add Auth0 authentication to a Python API application using the auth0-api-python SDK.

**Auth0 Credentials:**
- Domain: `dev-example.auth0.com`
- Client ID: `abc123def456ghi789jkl012`
- API Audience: `https://api.example.com`
evals.expected_output: Working Python API with Auth0 JWT validation, protected endpoints, and RBAC permissions
expectations: Auth0 SDK installed via any valid method (requirements.txt, pip install, or pyproject.toml)
Has correct import statement from auth0_api_python
Auth0 SDK initialized (ApiClient or ApiClientOptions created)
Configures JWT Bearer token validation using SDK methods
Configures API audience for token validation
Has protected API endpoint with auth guard
Auth0 domain dev-example.auth0.com written to .env config file
API audience https://api.example.com written to .env config file
No login UI methods or callback URLs in stateless API
No client secret in API config (uses JWKS public key validation)
Correctly integrates Auth0 JWT validation into Python API with protected endpoints and CORS
Uses current SDK version (1.0.0b8) in dependency declaration
Uses Auth0-specific error types from auth0_api_python.errors (not generic Exception)
Accesses permissions claim for RBAC authorization (not scope claim)
Uses SDK error method to return correct HTTP status code (get_status_code())
Does not use manual JWT decoding (uses auth0-api-python SDK instead of PyJWT/jose)
AUTH0_DOMAIN environment variable used in configuration
Applies skill-specific advanced patterns (error types, permissions claim, get_status_code)
